title: Find a GGUF tensor BY NAME + resolve its absolute data offset — the weight-addressing keystone, four-way proven
advances: form-native-models.form rung (1) toward real-model inference. The header parse (gguf-read.fk, PR row 'gguf-read fks 127'), the metadata-KV + tensor-info walk to a KNOWN offset (gguf-locate, 'gguf-locate fks 127'), and the dequant math for every quant type the real llama3.2:3b uses (q4k/q6k/f16, four-way) were all standing. The missing connective tissue between 'located a known tensor' and 'load a NAMED real weight' (blk.0.attn_q.weight, token_embd.weight, ...) was: (a) WALK the tensor-info table comparing names to find a target record, and (b) resolve a tensor's RELATIVE dataoffset to an ABSOLUTE file offset through the alignment-padded data-region base. This recipe adds both.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure integer/byte arithmetic band)
fixture: a hand-built TWO-tensor mini-GGUF (offsets hand-counted): GGUF v3, tensor_count=2, kv_count=2; KV 'a'=u32(42) @24, KV 'b'=array<u32>[7,8] @41, tensor 'wq' (Q4_K, dims=[256], dataoff=0) record @74, tensor 'wk' (Q4_K, dims=[128], dataoff=128) record @108, table end @142, data-region base = align_up(142,32) = 160. A two-tensor file is the minimum that makes find-by-name DISCRIMINATE rather than land on the only record. Logic proven four-way here; a carrier witnesses the same recipe on the real 2GB llama3.2:3b (which cannot be a band fixture).
recipe: form/form-stdlib/gguf-read.fk — appended: gg-bytes-eq / gg-name-eq (length-reject then byte-compare a record's name vs a target byte-list), gg-find / gg-find-tensor (walk the table, return the matching record offset or -1 = (sub 0 1), the base64/bmf-core absent sentinel — the engine never invents a match), gg-tinfo-end (walk past all tensor-info records to the table end), gg-align-up(x,a) = ((x + a-1) / a) * a (floor-div only, the proven q4k lane — no mod/bitwise primitive), gg-data-base (align_up of the table end), gg-tensor-abs (data-base + the record's relative dataoff = the absolute file byte offset of a named tensor's data, default alignment 32).
band: form/form-stdlib/tests/gguf-find-band.fk — 8 strange-minimal claims (verdict 255): bit1 find 'wq' -> record @74 (lands first record); bit2 find 'wk' -> @108 (DISCRIMINATES, not just the first); bit4 find 'wz' -> -1 (absent name, never invents); bit8 table end -> 142 (walk past BOTH records); bit16 data-base(32) -> 160 (alignment padding 142->160 fires when needed); bit32 abs('wq') -> 160 (base + dataoff 0); bit64 abs('wk') -> 288 (base + relative dataoff 128); bit128 align_up(128,32) -> 128 (an exact multiple is unchanged — padding only when needed).
reference: GGUF v3 spec (ggml): tensor data begins right after the tensor-info table, padded UP to general.alignment (default 32); each tensor's stored offset is RELATIVE to that base. The mini-GGUF offsets were hand-computed against gguf-read.fk's own readers (gg-str-next/gg-ti-tail/gg-ti-next) and confirmed by the band's own gg-tinfo-start (74) and gg-tinfo-end (142) claims.
proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/gguf-read.fk form-stdlib/tests/gguf-find-band.fk -> 255, fourth arm: 1 band four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 1 ok, 0 divergent. Regression: gguf-read-band and gguf-locate-band still cross at 127, 0 divergent (the append touched no existing function).
verdict: FOUR-WAY 255, 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all find a tensor by name, reject an absent name with -1, and resolve a named tensor's relative dataoffset to the same alignment-padded absolute file offset.
reading: With gg-find-tensor + gg-tensor-abs, a Form recipe can now name a real weight ('blk.0.attn_q.weight') and compute exactly where its bytes live in the .gguf file — the addressing the proven dequant (q4k/q6k/f16) and the four-way GQA decode stack (#3616/#3620) consume. Only read_file_bytes (host I/O, like the mic/camera carrier) sits between this and loading a real llama3.2:3b weight to f32 entirely in Form. Complements codex PR #3669 (form-cli GGUF model-cell), which builds the form-cli model-cell layer ABOVE this recipe-level keystone. Named next stones: a carrier that reads the real 2GB file's bytes and witnesses gg-tensor-abs against gguf's own tensor offsets; then load + dequant a named real weight to f32 in Form; then feed it through the four-way GQA decode loop (#3654) -> the first end-to-end native local llama token.
reproduce: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/gguf-read.fk form-stdlib/tests/gguf-find-band.fk -> 255 four-way; the band fixture is self-contained (hand-built mini-GGUF byte list), no external file needed.
note: All ops are div/mul/add/sub/nth/len/eq over a byte list — the fourth-friendly arithmetic subset, no host I/O, no floats (no scientific-notation-literal risk), small tables. The -1 absent sentinel uses (sub 0 1), confirmed four-way by base64 (fks 12). Lane held (READ-THE-BODY): grepped form/ first — header parse, locate-at-known-offset, and dequant math all existed, but find-by-name and the aligned data-base were a genuine gap; design (the GGUF alignment rule + the two-tensor discriminating fixture + the hand-counted offsets) was the cost, the four-way proof was seconds.
